x-enumeration-literals.name: Fintech Partner
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Technology-driven companies that provide digital financial solutions.

Comment:
Example: A bank partners with a fintech to offer instant digital loans or AI-powered investment advice.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Vendor / Service Provider
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Companies that provide operational or technological services to the bank.

Comment:
A bank uses a cloud service provider for core banking system hosting.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Insurance Partners (Bancassurance)
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Insurance companies that collaborate with banks to distribute insurance products.

Comment:
Example: A bank sells home insurance policies on behalf of an insurance company.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Retail / Co-Branding Partner
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Retailers or consumer brands that collaborate with banks for co-branded financial products.

Comment:
Example: A supermarket chain issues a co-branded credit card with a bank for cashback rewards.

x-enumeration-literals.name: International Banking Partner
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Other banks, often in different countries, that provide services where the bank has no physical presence.

Comment:
Example: A U.S. bank partners with an Indian bank to process international remittances.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Compliance Partner
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specialized firms that assist banks in meeting regulatory, audit, and compliance requirements.

Comment:
Example: A bank engages a compliance software provider for real-time transaction monitoring.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Technology & API Partner
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Providers of APIs, software platforms, or digital ecosystems that integrate with the banks systems.

Comment:
Example: A ride-sharing app integrates banking APIs to provide in-app wallets or instant driver payouts.
